samhitapatha: अ॒ग्निमी॑ळे पु॒रोहि॑तं य॒ज्ञस्य॑ दे॒वमृत्विज॑म् । होता॑रं रत्न॒धात॑मम् ॥

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: I praise Agni—the foremost priest placed at the heart of the sacrifice, the radiant divine minister, the grand invoker who bestows the highest treasures.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: अग्निनामकं देवम् ईळे स्तौमि । यज्ञस्य पुरोहितं — यथा राज्ञः पुरोहितस्तदभीष्टं संपादयति, तथाग्निरपि यज्ञस्यापेक्षितं होमं संपादयति । यद्वा, यज्ञस्य संबन्धिनि पूर्वभागे आहवनीयरूपेणावस्थितम् । देवं दानादिगुणयुक्तम् । होतारम् ऋत्विजम् — देवानां यज्ञेषु होतृनामक ऋत्विगग्निरेव; तथा च श्रूयते — "अग्निर्वै देवानां होता" इति । रत्नधातमं — यागफलरूपाणां रत्नानामतिशयेन धारयितारं पोषयितारं वा ।

samhitapatha: अ॒ग्निः पूर्वे॑भि॒र्ऋषि॑भि॒रीड्यो॒ नूत॑नैरु॒त । स दे॒वाँ एह व॑क्षति ॥

padapatha.grammar: verb, aorist subj, active, 3rd pers, sg

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: Agni, worthy of invocation by the ancient seers and by the new alike—may he bring the gods hither.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: अयम् अग्निः पूर्वेभिः पुरातनैर्भृग्वङ्गिरःप्रभृतिभिः ऋषिभिः ईड्यः स्तुत्यः; नूतनैः उत — इदानींतनैरस्माभिरपि स्तुत्यः । सः अग्निः स्तुतः सन् इह यज्ञे देवान् हविर्भुजः आ वक्षति — आवहतु इत्यर्थः ।

samhitapatha: अ॒ग्निना॑ र॒यिम॑श्नव॒त्पोष॑मे॒व दि॒वेदि॑वे । य॒शसं॑ वी॒रव॑त्तमम् ॥

padapatha.grammar: verb, present subj, active, 3rd pers, sg

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: Through Agni, one attains prosperity and nourishment that increases day by day, replete with glory and the highest heroic strength.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: योऽयं होत्रा स्तुत्योऽग्निस्तेन अग्निना निमित्तभूतेन यजमानः रयिं धनम् अश्नवत् — प्राप्नोति । कीदृशं रयिम् — दिवेदिवे पोषम् एव — प्रतिदिनं पुष्यमाणतया वर्धमानमेव, न तु कदाचिदपि क्षीयमाणम् । यशसं — दानादिना यशोयुक्तम् । वीरवत्तमम् — अतिशयेन पुत्रभृत्यादिवीरपुरुषोपेतम् । सति हि धने पुरुषाः संपद्यन्ते ।

samhitapatha: अग्ने॒ यं य॒ज्ञम॑ध्व॒रं वि॒श्वतः॑ परि॒भूरसि॑ । स इद्दे॒वेषु॑ गच्छति ॥

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: O Agni, the flawless sacrifice that you encompass on all sides—that alone reaches the gods.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: हे अग्ने, त्वं यं यज्ञं विश्वतः सर्वासु दिक्षु परिभूः — परितः प्राप्तवान् असि, सः इत् — स एव यज्ञः देवेषु तृप्तिं प्रणेतुं स्वर्गे गच्छति । प्राच्यादिचतुर्दिगन्तेषु आहवनीयमार्जालीयगार्हपत्याग्नीध्रीयस्थानेषु अग्निरस्ति; परिशब्देन होत्रीयादिधिष्ण्यव्याप्तिर्विवक्षिता । कीदृशं यज्ञम् — अध्वरं हिंसारहितम् — न ह्यग्निना सर्वतः पालितं यज्ञं राक्षसादयो हिंसितुं प्रभवन्ति ।

samhitapatha: अ॒ग्निर्होता॑ क॒विक्र॑तुः स॒त्यश्चि॒त्रश्र॑वस्तमः । दे॒वो दे॒वेभि॒रा ग॑मत् ॥

padapatha.literal_meaning: of seer-like will / possessed of poetic wisdom

padapatha.grammar: verb, aorist inj, active, 3rd pers, sg

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: May Agni, the invoker of seer-like will, true and most brilliantly famed, come hither—a god alongside the gods.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: अग्निः होता — यज्ञे हविर्देवेभ्यो ददाति इति होता । कविक्रतुः — कवेः क्रान्तदर्शिनः मेधाविनो वा क्रतुः — प्रज्ञा यस्य स कविक्रतुः; दीप्तप्रज्ञ इत्यर्थः । सत्यः — यज्ञफलप्रदाने सत्यसंकल्पः । चित्रश्रवस्तमः — आश्चर्यकरतमशब्दः — अत्यन्तविचित्रयशस्क इत्यर्थः । सः देवः देवेभिः — अन्यैर्वसुरुद्रादिभिः देवैः सह — इह यज्ञे आ गमत् — आगच्छतु ।

samhitapatha: यद॒ङ्ग दा॒शुषे॒ त्वमग्ने॑ भ॒द्रं क॑रि॒ष्यसि॑ । तवेत्तत्स॒त्यम॑ङ्गिरः ॥

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: Whatever good you shall bestow upon the offerer, O Agni, that truth is inherently yours, O Angiras.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: हे अङ्गिरः अग्ने, यत् अङ्ग — यस्मात्कारणात् त्वम् दाशुषे — हविर्दात्रे यजमानाय भद्रं कल्याणम् — धनधान्यादिकं करिष्यसि — करोषि, तवेत् तत् — तव एव तत्, सत्यम् — नियतं भवतीत्यर्थः । त्वत्कृतं भद्रं सत्यमेव, न कदाचिद्विफलं भवतीति भावः ।

samhitapatha: उप॑ त्वाग्ने दि॒वेदि॑वे॒ दोषा॑वस्तर्धि॒या व॒यम् । नमो॒ भर॑न्त॒ एम॑सि ॥

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: To you, O Agni, illuminator of the dusk, we approach day by day, bearing our homage through devotion.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: उप त्वा — उपसमीपम् अग्ने — वयम् प्रतिदिनम् अध्वरे — यज्ञे नमोभिः — नमस्कारैः जुह्वत् — होमं कुर्वत् आ ईमहे — याचामहे स्तुमश्च । राजन्तम् — देदीप्यमानम् । अध्वराणाम् — यज्ञानाम् गोपाम् — रक्षितारम् । ऋतस्य — यज्ञस्य, सत्यस्य वा दीदिविम् — दीप्तिमन्तम् । अपां नपातम् — अपः प्रशंसार्थे, नपात् — जातम्; अत्यन्तं तेजस्विनमित्यर्थः ।

samhitapatha: राज॑न्तमध्व॒राणां॑ गो॒पामृ॒तस्य॒ दीदि॑विम् । वर्ध॑मानं॒ स्वे दमे॑ ॥

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: Ruling over the sacrifices, shining as the guardian of the Cosmic Order, growing ever greater in your own dwelling.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: हे अग्ने, पिता न — पिता यथा पुत्रम् आयुं — आयुष्मन्तं जीवन्तम् सुपायनः — सुखेन प्राप्यः उप — उपसमीपम् एहि — आगच्छ । सखे — सख्यभावेन; हे मित्र इत्यर्थः । पितुर्यथा पुत्रान् पालयति, तद्वत् अस्मान् रक्ष ।

samhitapatha: स नः॑ पि॒तेव॑ सू॒नवेऽग्ने॑ सूपाय॒नो भ॑व । सच॑स्वा नः स्व॒स्तये॑ ॥

translation.author: Sachit Varshney
translation.fluid_text: Be easy of approach to us, O Agni, just as a father is to his son. Abide with us for our well-being.

commentary.author: Sāyaṇa
commentary.text: हे अग्ने, यत् अर्हसि — यस्मात्त्वं पूज्यः, तस्मात् सः — त्वम् श्रेष्ठतमः अर्हसि — अस्माभिः पूजनीयोऽसि । रात्री — रात्रिषु — क्षपाभिः — रात्रिभिः अपि, दिवा — दिवसेऽपि देव वन्तो — भजमानाः धीमहि — ध्याये म — अर्चेम । इह — अस्मिन् यज्ञे नः — अस्माकम् उ लोकधातु — लोकनिर्मातारः सुहवाः — सुष्ठु आहूयमानाः — देवाः गमन्तु ।

samhitapatha: वाय॒वा या॑हि दर्शते॒मे सोमा॒ अरं॑कृताः । तेषां॑ पाहि श्रु॒धी हव॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: BEAUTIFUL Vāyu, come, for thee these Soma drops have been prepared: Drink of them, hearken to our call.

samhitapatha: वाय॑ उ॒क्थेभि॑र्जरन्ते॒ त्वामच्छा॑ जरि॒तारः॑ । सु॒तसो॑मा अह॒र्विदः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Knowing the days, with Soma juice poured forth, the singers glorify Thee, Vāyu, with their hymns of praise.

samhitapatha: वायो॒ तव॑ प्रपृञ्च॒ती धेना॑ जिगाति दा॒शुषे॑ । उ॒रू॒ची सोम॑पीतये ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Vāyu, thy penetrating stream goes forth unto the worshipper, Far-spreading for the Soma draught.

samhitapatha: इन्द्र॑वायू इ॒मे सु॒ता उप॒ प्रयो॑भि॒रा ग॑तम् । इन्द॑वो वामु॒शन्ति॒ हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: These, Indra-Vāyu, have been shed; come for our offered dainties sake: The drops are yearning for you both.

samhitapatha: वाय॒विन्द्र॑श्च चेतथः सु॒तानां॑ वाजिनीवसू । तावा या॑त॒मुप॑ द्र॒वत् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Well do ye mark libations, ye Vāyu and Indra, rich in spoil! So come ye swiftly hitherward.

samhitapatha: वाय॒विन्द्र॑श्च सुन्व॒त आ या॑त॒मुप॑ निष्कृ॒तम् । म॒क्ष्वि१॒॑त्था धि॒या न॑रा ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Vāyu and Indra, come to what the Soma-presser hath prepared: Soon, Heroes, thus I make my prayer.

samhitapatha: मि॒त्रं हु॑वे पू॒तद॑क्षं॒ वरु॑णं च रि॒शाद॑सम् । धियं॑ घृ॒ताचीं॒ साध॑न्ता ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Mitra, of holy strength, I call, and foe-destroying Varuṇa, Who make the oil-fed rite complete.

samhitapatha: ऋ॒तेन॑ मित्रावरुणावृतावृधावृतस्पृशा । क्रतुं॑ बृ॒हन्त॑माशाथे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Mitra and Varuṇa, through Law, lovers and cherishers of Law, Have ye obtained your might power

samhitapatha: क॒वी नो॑ मि॒त्रावरु॑णा तुविजा॒ता उ॑रु॒क्षया॑ । दक्षं॑ दधाते अ॒पस॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Our Sages, Mitra-Varuṇa, wide dominion, strong by birth, Vouchsafe us strength that worketh well.

samhitapatha: अश्वि॑ना॒ यज्व॑री॒रिषो॒ द्रव॑त्पाणी॒ शुभ॑स्पती । पुरु॑भुजा चन॒स्यत॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: YE Aśvins, rich in treasure, Lords of splendour, having nimble hands, Accept the sacrificial food.

samhitapatha: अश्वि॑ना॒ पुरु॑दंससा॒ नरा॒ शवी॑रया धि॒या । धिष्ण्या॒ वन॑तं॒ गिरः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Ye Aśvins, rich in wondrous deeds, ye heroes worthy of our praise, Accept our songs with mighty thought.

samhitapatha: दस्रा॑ यु॒वाक॑वः सु॒ता नास॑त्या वृ॒क्तब॑र्हिषः । आ या॑तं रुद्रवर्तनी ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Nāsatyas, wonder-workers, yours are these libations with clipt grass: Come ye whose paths are red with flame.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रा या॑हि चित्रभानो सु॒ता इ॒मे त्वा॒यवः॑ । अण्वी॑भि॒स्तना॑ पू॒तासः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra marvellously bright, come, these libations long for thee, Thus by fine fingers purified.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रा या॑हि धि॒येषि॒तो विप्र॑जूतः सु॒ताव॑तः । उप॒ ब्रह्मा॑णि वा॒घतः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Urged by the holy singer, sped by song, come, Indra, to the prayers, Of the libation-pouring priest.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रा या॑हि॒ तूतु॑जान॒ उप॒ ब्रह्मा॑णि हरिवः । सु॒ते द॑धिष्व न॒श्चनः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Approach, O Indra, hasting thee, Lord of Bay Horses, to the prayers. In our libation take delight.

samhitapatha: ओमा॑सश्चर्षणीधृतो॒ विश्वे॑ देवास॒ आ ग॑त । दा॒श्वांसो॑ दा॒शुषः॑ सु॒तम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Ye Viśvedevas, who protect, reward, and cherish men, approach Your worshipper's drink-offering.

samhitapatha: विश्वे॑ दे॒वासो॑ अ॒प्तुरः॑ सु॒तमा ग॑न्त॒ तूर्ण॑यः । उ॒स्रा इ॑व॒ स्वस॑राणि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Ye Viśvedevas, swift at work, come hither quickly to the draught, As milch-kine hasten to their stalls.

samhitapatha: विश्वे॑ दे॒वासो॑ अ॒स्रिध॒ एहि॑मायासो अ॒द्रुहः॑ । मेधं॑ जुषन्त॒ वह्न॑यः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The Viśvedevas, changing shape like serpents, fearless, void of guile, Bearers, accept the sacred draught

samhitapatha: पा॒व॒का नः॒ सर॑स्वती॒ वाजे॑भिर्वा॒जिनी॑वती । य॒ज्ञं व॑ष्टु धि॒याव॑सुः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Wealthy in spoil, enriched with hymns, may bright Sarasvatī desire, With eager love, our sacrifice.

samhitapatha: चो॒द॒यि॒त्री सू॒नृता॑नां॒ चेत॑न्ती सुमती॒नाम् । य॒ज्ञं द॑धे॒ सर॑स्वती ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Inciter of all pleasant songs, inspirer of all gracious thought, Sarasvatī accept our rite

samhitapatha: म॒हो अर्णः॒ सर॑स्वती॒ प्र चे॑तयति के॒तुना॑ । धियो॒ विश्वा॒ वि रा॑जति ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Sarasvatī, the mighty flood,she with her light illuminates, She brightens every pious thought.

samhitapatha: सु॒रू॒प॒कृ॒त्नुमू॒तये॑ सु॒दुघा॑मिव गो॒दुहे॑ । जु॒हू॒मसि॒ द्यवि॑द्यवि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: As a good cow to him who milks, we call the doer of fair deeds, To our assistance day by day.

samhitapatha: उप॑ नः॒ सव॒ना ग॑हि॒ सोम॑स्य सोमपाः पिब । गो॒दा इद्रे॒वतो॒ मदः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Come thou to our libations, drink of Soma; Soma-drinker thou! The rich One's rapture giveth kine.

samhitapatha: अथा॑ ते॒ अन्त॑मानां वि॒द्याम॑ सुमती॒नाम् । मा नो॒ अति॑ ख्य॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: So may we be acquainted with thine innermost benevolence: Neglect us not, come hitherward.

samhitapatha: परे॑हि॒ विग्र॒मस्तृ॑त॒मिन्द्रं॑ पृच्छा विप॒श्चित॑म् । यस्ते॒ सखि॑भ्य॒ आ वर॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Go to the wise unconquered One, ask thou of Indra, skilled in song, Him who is better than thy friends.

samhitapatha: उ॒त ब्रु॑वन्तु नो॒ निदो॒ निर॒न्यत॑श्चिदारत । दधा॑ना॒ इन्द्र॒ इद्दुवः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Whether the men who mock us say, Depart unto another place, Ye who serve Indra and none else;

samhitapatha: उ॒त नः॑ सु॒भगाँ॑ अ॒रिर्वो॒चेयु॑र्दस्म कृ॒ष्टयः॑ । स्यामेदिन्द्र॑स्य॒ शर्म॑णि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Or whether, God of wondrous deeds, all our true people call us blest, Still may we dwell in Indra's care.

samhitapatha: एमा॒शुमा॒शवे॑ भर यज्ञ॒श्रियं॑ नृ॒माद॑नम् । प॒त॒यन्म॑न्द॒यत्स॑खम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Unto the swift One bring the swift, man-cheering, grace of sacrifice, That to the Friend gives wings and joy.

samhitapatha: अ॒स्य पी॒त्वा श॑तक्रतो घ॒नो वृ॒त्राणा॑मभवः । प्रावो॒ वाजे॑षु वा॒जिन॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Thou, Śatakratu, drankest this and wast the Vṛtras slayer; thou Helpest the warrior in the fray.

samhitapatha: तं त्वा॒ वाजे॑षु वा॒जिनं॑ वा॒जया॑मः शतक्रतो । धना॑नामिन्द्र सा॒तये॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: We strengthen, Śatakratu, thee, yea, thee the powerful in fight, That, Indra, we may win us wealth.

samhitapatha: यो रा॒यो॒३॒॑ऽवनि॑र्म॒हान्त्सु॑पा॒रः सु॑न्व॒तः सखा॑ । तस्मा॒ इन्द्रा॑य गायत ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To him the mighty stream of wealth, prompt friend of him who pours the juice, Yea, to this Indra sing your song.

samhitapatha: आ त्वेता॒ नि षी॑द॒तेन्द्र॑म॒भि प्र गा॑यत । सखा॑यः॒ स्तोम॑वाहसः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O COME ye hither, sit ye down: to Indra sing ye forth, your song, companions, bringing hymns of praise.

samhitapatha: पु॒रू॒तमं॑ पुरू॒णामीशा॑नं॒ वार्या॑णाम् । इन्द्रं॒ सोमे॒ सचा॑ सु॒ते ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To him the richest of the rich, the Lord of treasures excellent, Indra, with Soma juice outpoured.

samhitapatha: स घा॑ नो॒ योग॒ आ भु॑व॒त्स रा॒ये स पुरं॑ध्याम् । गम॒द्वाजे॑भि॒रा स नः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: May he stand by us in our need and in abundance for our wealth: May he come nigh us with his strength.

samhitapatha: यस्य॑ सं॒स्थे न वृ॒ण्वते॒ हरी॑ स॒मत्सु॒ शत्र॑वः । तस्मा॒ इन्द्रा॑य गायत ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Whose pair of tawny horses yoked in battles foemen challenge not: To him, to Indra sing your song.

samhitapatha: सु॒त॒पाव्ने॑ सु॒ता इ॒मे शुच॑यो यन्ति वी॒तये॑ । सोमा॑सो॒ दध्या॑शिरः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Nigh to the Soma-drinker come, for his enjoyment, these pure drops, The Somas mingled with the curd.

samhitapatha: त्वं सु॒तस्य॑ पी॒तये॑ स॒द्यो वृ॒द्धो अ॑जायथाः । इन्द्र॒ ज्यैष्ठ्या॑य सुक्रतो ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Thou, grown at once to perfect strength, wast born to drink the Soma juice, Strong Indra, for preëminence.

samhitapatha: आ त्वा॑ विशन्त्वा॒शवः॒ सोमा॑स इन्द्र गिर्वणः । शं ते॑ सन्तु॒ प्रचे॑तसे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra, lover of the song, may these quick Somas enter thee: May they bring bliss to thee the Sage.

samhitapatha: त्वां स्तोमा॑ अवीवृध॒न्त्वामु॒क्था श॑तक्रतो । त्वां व॑र्धन्तु नो॒ गिरः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Our chants of praise have strengthened thee, O Śatakratu, and our lauds So strengthen thee the songs we sing.

samhitapatha: अक्षि॑तोतिः सनेदि॒मं वाज॒मिन्द्रः॑ सह॒स्रिण॑म् । यस्मि॒न्विश्वा॑नि॒ पौंस्या॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra, whose succour never fails, accept these viands thousandfold, Wherein all manly powers abide.

samhitapatha: मा नो॒ मर्ता॑ अ॒भि द्रु॑हन्त॒नूना॑मिन्द्र गिर्वणः । ईशा॑नो यवया व॒धम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra, thou who lovest song, let no man hurt our bodies, keep Slaughter far from us, for thou canst.

samhitapatha: यु॒ञ्जन्ति॑ ब्र॒ध्नम॑रु॒षं चर॑न्तं॒ परि॑ त॒स्थुषः॑ । रोच॑न्ते रोच॒ना दि॒वि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: They who stand round him as he moves harness the bright, the ruddy Steed The lights are shining in the sky.

samhitapatha: यु॒ञ्जन्त्य॑स्य॒ काम्या॒ हरी॒ विप॑क्षसा॒ रथे॑ । शोणा॑ धृ॒ष्णू नृ॒वाह॑सा ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: On both sides to the car they yoke the two bay coursers dear to him, Bold, tawny, bearers of the Chief.

samhitapatha: के॒तुं कृ॒ण्वन्न॑के॒तवे॒ पेशो॑ मर्या अपे॒शसे॑ । समु॒षद्भि॑रजायथाः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Thou, making light where no light was, and form, O men: where form was not, Wast born together with the Dawns.

samhitapatha: आदह॑ स्व॒धामनु॒ पुन॑र्गर्भ॒त्वमे॑रि॒रे । दधा॑ना॒ नाम॑ य॒ज्ञिय॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Thereafter they, as is their wont, threw off the state of babes unborn, Assuming sacrificial names.

samhitapatha: वी॒ळु चि॑दारुज॒त्नुभि॒र्गुहा॑ चिदिन्द्र॒ वह्नि॑भिः । अवि॑न्द उ॒स्रिया॒ अनु॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Thou, Indra, with the Tempest-Gods, the breakers down of what is firm, Foundest the kine even in the cave.

samhitapatha: दे॒व॒यन्तो॒ यथा॑ म॒तिमच्छा॑ वि॒दद्व॑सुं॒ गिरः॑ । म॒हाम॑नूषत श्रु॒तम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Worshipping even as they list, singers laud him who findeth wealth, The far-renowned, the mighty One.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रे॑ण॒ सं हि दृक्ष॑से संजग्मा॒नो अबि॑भ्युषा । म॒न्दू स॑मा॒नव॑र्चसा ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Mayest thou verily be seen coming by fearless Indra's side: Both joyous, equal in your sheen.

samhitapatha: अ॒न॒व॒द्यैर॒भिद्यु॑भिर्म॒खः सह॑स्वदर्चति । ग॒णैरिन्द्र॑स्य॒ काम्यैः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: With Indra's well beloved hosts, the blameless, hastening to heaven, The sacrificer cries aloud.

samhitapatha: अतः॑ परिज्म॒न्ना ग॑हि दि॒वो वा॑ रोच॒नादधि॑ । सम॑स्मिन्नृञ्जते॒ गिरः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Come from this place, O Wanderer, or downward from the light of heaven: Our songs of praise all yearn for this.

samhitapatha: इ॒तो वा॑ सा॒तिमीम॑हे दि॒वो वा॒ पार्थि॑वा॒दधि॑ । इन्द्रं॑ म॒हो वा॒ रज॑सः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra we seek to give us help, from here, from heaven above the earth, Or from the spacious firmament.

samhitapatha: इन्द्र॒मिद्गा॒थिनो॑ बृ॒हदिन्द्र॑म॒र्केभि॑र॒र्किणः॑ । इन्द्रं॒ वाणी॑रनूषत ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: INDRA the singers with high praise, Indra reciters with their lauds, Indra the choirs have glorified.

samhitapatha: इन्द्र॒ इद्धर्योः॒ सचा॒ संमि॑श्ल॒ आ व॑चो॒युजा॑ । इन्द्रो॑ व॒ज्री हि॑र॒ण्ययः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra hath ever close to him his two bay steeds and word-yoked car, Indra the golden, thunder-armed.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रो॑ दी॒र्घाय॒ चक्ष॑स॒ आ सूर्यं॑ रोहयद्दि॒वि । वि गोभि॒रद्रि॑मैरयत् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra hath raised the Sun on high in heaven, that he may see afar: He burst the mountain for the kine.

samhitapatha: इन्द्र॒ वाजे॑षु नोऽव स॒हस्र॑प्रधनेषु च । उ॒ग्र उ॒ग्राभि॑रू॒तिभिः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Help us, O Indra, in the frays, yea, frays, where thousand spoils are gained, With awful aids, O awful One.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रं॑ व॒यं म॑हाध॒न इन्द्र॒मर्भे॑ हवामहे । युजं॑ वृ॒त्रेषु॑ व॒ज्रिण॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: In mighty battle we invoke Indra, Indra in lesser fight, The Friend who bends his bolt at fiends.

samhitapatha: स नो॑ वृषन्न॒मुं च॒रुं सत्रा॑दाव॒न्नपा॑ वृधि । अ॒स्मभ्य॒मप्र॑तिष्कुतः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Unclose, our manly Hero, thou for ever bounteous, yonder cloud, For us, thou irresistible.

samhitapatha: तु॒ञ्जेतु॑ञ्जे॒ य उत्त॑रे॒ स्तोमा॒ इन्द्र॑स्य व॒ज्रिणः॑ । न वि॑न्धे अस्य सुष्टु॒तिम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Still higher, at each strain of mine, thunder-armed Indra's praises rise: I find no laud worthy of him.

samhitapatha: वृषा॑ यू॒थेव॒ वंस॑गः कृ॒ष्टीरि॑य॒र्त्योज॑सा । ईशा॑नो॒ अप्र॑तिष्कुतः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Even as the bull drives on the herds, he drives the people with his might, The Ruler irresistible:

samhitapatha: य एक॑श्चर्षणी॒नां वसू॑नामिर॒ज्यति॑ । इन्द्रः॒ पञ्च॑ क्षिती॒नाम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra who rules with single sway men, riches, and the fivefold race Of those who dwell upon the earth.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रं॑ वो वि॒श्वत॒स्परि॒ हवा॑महे॒ जने॑भ्यः । अ॒स्माक॑मस्तु॒ केव॑लः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: For your sake from each side we call Indra away from other men: Ours, and none others, may he be.

samhitapatha: एन्द्र॑ सान॒सिं र॒यिं स॒जित्वा॑नं सदा॒सह॑म् । वर्षि॑ष्ठमू॒तये॑ भर ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: INDRA, bring wealth that gives delight, the victor's ever-conquering wealth, Most excellent, to be our aid;

samhitapatha: नि येन॑ मुष्टिह॒त्यया॒ नि वृ॒त्रा रु॒णधा॑महै । त्वोता॑सो॒ न्यर्व॑ता ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: By means of which we may repel our foes in battle hand to hand, By thee assisted with the car.

samhitapatha: इन्द्र॒ त्वोता॑स॒ आ व॒यं वज्रं॑ घ॒ना द॑दीमहि । जये॑म॒ सं यु॒धि स्पृधः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Aided by thee, the thunder-armed, Indra, may we lift up the bolt, And conquer all our foes in fight.

samhitapatha: व॒यं शूरे॑भि॒रस्तृ॑भि॒रिन्द्र॒ त्वया॑ यु॒जा व॒यम् । सा॒स॒ह्याम॑ पृतन्य॒तः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: With thee, O India, for ally with missile-darting heroes, may We conquer our embattled foes.

samhitapatha: म॒हाँ इन्द्रः॑ प॒रश्च॒ नु म॑हि॒त्वम॑स्तु व॒ज्रिणे॑ । द्यौर्न प्र॑थि॒ना शवः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Mighty is Indra, yea supreme; greatness be his, the Thunderer: Wide as the heaven extends his power

samhitapatha: स॒मो॒हे वा॒ य आश॑त॒ नर॑स्तो॒कस्य॒ सनि॑तौ । विप्रा॑सो वा धिया॒यवः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Which aideth those to win them sons, who come as heroes to the fight, Or singers loving holy thoughts.

samhitapatha: यः कु॒क्षिः सो॑म॒पात॑मः समु॒द्र इ॑व॒ पिन्व॑ते । उ॒र्वीरापो॒ न का॒कुदः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: His belly, drinking deepest draughts of Soma, like an ocean swells, Like wide streams from the cope of heaven.

samhitapatha: ए॒वा ह्य॑स्य सू॒नृता॑ विर॒प्शी गोम॑ती म॒ही । प॒क्वा शाखा॒ न दा॒शुषे॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: So also is his excellence, great, vigorous, rich in cattle, like A ripe branch to the worshipper.

samhitapatha: ए॒वा हि ते॒ विभू॑तय ऊ॒तय॑ इन्द्र॒ माव॑ते । स॒द्यश्चि॒त्सन्ति॑ दा॒शुषे॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: For verily thy mighty powers, Indra, are saving helps at once Unto a worshipper like me.

samhitapatha: ए॒वा ह्य॑स्य॒ काम्या॒ स्तोम॑ उ॒क्थं च॒ शंस्या॑ । इन्द्रा॑य॒ सोम॑पीतये ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: So are his lovely gifts; let lauds and praises be to Indra sung, That he may drink the Soma juice.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रेहि॒ मत्स्यन्ध॑सो॒ विश्वे॑भिः सोम॒पर्व॑भिः । म॒हाँ अ॑भि॒ष्टिरोज॑सा ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: COME, Indra, and delight thee with the juice at all the Soma feasts, Protector, mighty in thy strength.

samhitapatha: एमे॑नं सृजता सु॒ते म॒न्दिमिन्द्रा॑य म॒न्दिने॑ । चक्रिं॒ विश्वा॑नि॒ चक्र॑ये ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To Indra pour ye forth the juice, the active gladdening juice to him The gladdening, omnific God.

samhitapatha: मत्स्वा॑ सुशिप्र म॒न्दिभिः॒ स्तोमे॑भिर्विश्वचर्षणे । सचै॒षु सव॑ने॒ष्वा ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Lord of all men, fair of cheek, rejoice thee in the gladdening lauds, Present at these drink-offerings.

samhitapatha: असृ॑ग्रमिन्द्र ते॒ गिरः॒ प्रति॒ त्वामुद॑हासत । अजो॑षा वृष॒भं पति॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Songs have outpoured themselves to thee, Indra, the strong, the guardian Lord, And raised themselves unsatisfied.

samhitapatha: सं चो॑दय चि॒त्रम॒र्वाग्राध॑ इन्द्र॒ वरे॑ण्यम् । अस॒दित्ते॑ वि॒भु प्र॒भु ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Send to us bounty manifold, O Indra, worthy of our wish, For power supreme is only thine.

samhitapatha: अ॒स्मान्त्सु तत्र॑ चोद॒येन्द्र॑ रा॒ये रभ॑स्वतः । तुवि॑द्युम्न॒ यश॑स्वतः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra, stimulate thereto us emulously fain for wealth, And glorious, O most splendid One.

samhitapatha: सं गोम॑दिन्द्र॒ वाज॑वद॒स्मे पृ॒थु श्रवो॑ बृ॒हत् । वि॒श्वायु॑र्धे॒ह्यक्षि॑तम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Give, Indra, wide and lofty fame, wealthy in cattle and in strength, Lasting our life-time, failing not.

samhitapatha: अ॒स्मे धे॑हि॒ श्रवो॑ बृ॒हद्द्यु॒म्नं स॑हस्र॒सात॑मम् । इन्द्र॒ ता र॒थिनी॒रिषः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Grant us high fame, O Indra, grant riches bestowing thousands, those Fair fruits of earth borne home in wains.

samhitapatha: वसो॒रिन्द्रं॒ वसु॑पतिं गी॒र्भिर्गृ॒णन्त॑ ऋ॒ग्मिय॑म् । होम॒ गन्ता॑रमू॒तये॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Praising with songs the praise-worthy who cometh to our aid, we call Indra, the Treasure-Lord of wealth.

samhitapatha: सु॒तेसु॑ते॒ न्यो॑कसे बृ॒हद्बृ॑ह॒त एद॒रिः । इन्द्रा॑य शू॒षम॑र्चति ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To lofty Indra, dweller by each libation, the pious man Sings forth aloud a strengthening hymn.

samhitapatha: गाय॑न्ति त्वा गाय॒त्रिणोऽर्च॑न्त्य॒र्कम॒र्किणः॑ । ब्र॒ह्माण॑स्त्वा शतक्रत॒ उद्वं॒शमि॑व येमिरे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: THE chanters hymn thee, they who say the word of praise magnify thee. The priests have raised thee up on high, O Śatakratu, like a pole.

samhitapatha: यत्सानोः॒ सानु॒मारु॑ह॒द्भूर्यस्प॑ष्ट॒ कर्त्व॑म् । तदिन्द्रो॒ अर्थं॑ चेतति यू॒थेन॑ वृ॒ष्णिरे॑जति ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: As up he clomb from ridge to ridge and looked upon the toilsome task, Indra observes this wish of his, and the Rain hastens with his troop.

samhitapatha: यु॒क्ष्वा हि के॒शिना॒ हरी॒ वृष॑णा कक्ष्य॒प्रा । अथा॑ न इन्द्र सोमपा गि॒रामुप॑श्रुतिं चर ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Harness thy pair of strong bay steeds, long-maned, whose bodies fill the girths, And, Indra, Soma-drinker, come to listen to our songs of praise.

samhitapatha: एहि॒ स्तोमाँ॑ अ॒भि स्व॑रा॒भि गृ॑णी॒ह्या रु॑व । ब्रह्म॑ च नो वसो॒ सचेन्द्र॑ य॒ज्ञं च॑ वर्धय ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Come hither, answer thou the song, sing in approval, cry aloud. Good Indra, make our prayer succeed, and prosper this our sacrifice.

samhitapatha: उ॒क्थमिन्द्रा॑य॒ शंस्यं॒ वर्ध॑नं पुरुनि॒ष्षिधे॑ । श॒क्रो यथा॑ सु॒तेषु॑ णो रा॒रण॑त्स॒ख्येषु॑ च ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To Indra must a laud be said, to strengthen him who freely gives, That Śakra may take pleasure in our friendship and drink-offerings.

samhitapatha: तमित्स॑खि॒त्व ई॑महे॒ तं रा॒ये तं सु॒वीर्ये॑ । स श॒क्र उ॒त नः॑ शक॒दिन्द्रो॒ वसु॒ दय॑मानः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Him, him we seek for friendship, him for riches and heroic might. For Indra, he is Śakra, he shall aid us while he gives us wealth.

samhitapatha: सु॒वि॒वृतं॑ सुनि॒रज॒मिन्द्र॒ त्वादा॑त॒मिद्यशः॑ । गवा॒मप॑ व्र॒जं वृ॑धि कृणु॒ष्व राधो॑ अद्रिवः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Easy to turn and drive away, Indra, is spoil bestowed by thee. Unclose the stable of the kine, and give us wealth O Thunder-armed

samhitapatha: न॒हि त्वा॒ रोद॑सी उ॒भे ऋ॑घा॒यमा॑ण॒मिन्व॑तः । जेषः॒ स्व॑र्वतीर॒पः सं गा अ॒स्मभ्यं॑ धूनुहि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The heaven and earth contain thee not, together, in thy wrathful mood. Win us the waters of the sky, and send us kine abundantly.

samhitapatha: आश्रु॑त्कर्ण श्रु॒धी हवं॒ नू चि॑द्दधिष्व मे॒ गिरः॑ । इन्द्र॒ स्तोम॑मि॒मं मम॑ कृ॒ष्वा यु॒जश्चि॒दन्त॑रम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Hear, thou whose ear is quick, my call; take to thee readily my songs O Indra, let this laud of mine come nearer even than thy friend.

samhitapatha: वि॒द्मा हि त्वा॒ वृष॑न्तमं॒ वाजे॑षु हवन॒श्रुत॑म् । वृष॑न्तमस्य हूमह ऊ॒तिं स॑हस्र॒सात॑माम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: We know thee mightiest of all, in battles hearer of our cry. Of thee most mighty we invoke the aid that giveth thousandfold.

samhitapatha: आ तू न॑ इन्द्र कौशिक मन्दसा॒नः सु॒तं पि॑ब । नव्य॒मायुः॒ प्र सू ति॑र कृ॒धी स॑हस्र॒सामृषि॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra, Son of Kuśika, drink our libation with delight. Prolong our life anew, and cause the seer to win a thousand gifts.

samhitapatha: परि॑ त्वा गिर्वणो॒ गिर॑ इ॒मा भ॑वन्तु वि॒श्वतः॑ । वृ॒द्धायु॒मनु॒ वृद्ध॑यो॒ जुष्टा॑ भवन्तु॒ जुष्ट॑यः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Lover of song, may these our songs on every side encompass thee: Strengthening thee of lengthened life, may they be dear delights to thee.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रं॒ विश्वा॑ अवीवृधन्त्समु॒द्रव्य॑चसं॒ गिरः॑ । र॒थीत॑मं र॒थीनां॒ वाजा॑नां॒ सत्प॑तिं॒ पति॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: ALL sacred songs have magnified Indra expansive as the sea, The best of warriors borne on cars, the Lord, the very Lord of strength.

samhitapatha: स॒ख्ये त॑ इन्द्र वा॒जिनो॒ मा भे॑म शवसस्पते । त्वाम॒भि प्र णो॑नुमो॒ जेता॑र॒मप॑राजितम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Strong in thy friendship, Indra, Lord of power and might, we have no fear. We glorify with praises thee, the never-conquered conqueror.

samhitapatha: पू॒र्वीरिन्द्र॑स्य रा॒तयो॒ न वि द॑स्यन्त्यू॒तयः॑ । यदी॒ वाज॑स्य॒ गोम॑तः स्तो॒तृभ्यो॒ मंह॑ते म॒घम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The gifts of Indra from of old, his saving succours, never fail, When to the praise-singers he gives the boon of substance rich in kine.

samhitapatha: पु॒रां भि॒न्दुर्युवा॑ क॒विरमि॑तौजा अजायत । इन्द्रो॒ विश्व॑स्य॒ कर्म॑णो ध॒र्ता व॒ज्री पु॑रुष्टु॒तः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Crusher of forts, the young, the wise, of strength unmeasured, was he born Sustainer of each sacred rite, Indra, the Thunderer, much-extolled.

samhitapatha: त्वं व॒लस्य॒ गोम॒तोऽपा॑वरद्रिवो॒ बिल॑म् । त्वां दे॒वा अबि॑भ्युषस्तु॒ज्यमा॑नास आविषुः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Lord of the thunder, thou didst burst the cave of Vala rich in cows. The Gods came pressing to thy side, and free from terror aided thee,

samhitapatha: तवा॒हं शू॑र रा॒तिभिः॒ प्रत्या॑यं॒ सिन्धु॑मा॒वद॑न् । उपा॑तिष्ठन्त गिर्वणो वि॒दुष्टे॒ तस्य॑ का॒रवः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: I, Hero, through thy bounties am come to the flood addressing thee. Song-lover, here the singers stand and testify to thee thereof.

samhitapatha: मा॒याभि॑रिन्द्र मा॒यिनं॒ त्वं शुष्ण॒मवा॑तिरः । वि॒दुष्टे॒ तस्य॒ मेधि॑रा॒स्तेषां॒ श्रवां॒स्युत्ति॑र ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The wily Śuṣṇa, Indra! thou oerthrewest with thy wondrous powers. The wise beheld this deed of thine: now go beyond their eulogies.

samhitapatha: इन्द्र॒मीशा॑न॒मोज॑सा॒भि स्तोमा॑ अनूषत । स॒हस्रं॒ यस्य॑ रा॒तय॑ उ॒त वा॒ सन्ति॒ भूय॑सीः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Our songs of praise have glorified Indra who ruleth by his might, Whose precious gifts in thousands come, yea, even more abundantly.

samhitapatha: अ॒ग्निं दू॒तं वृ॑णीमहे॒ होता॑रं वि॒श्ववे॑दसम् । अ॒स्य य॒ज्ञस्य॑ सु॒क्रतु॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: WE choose Agni the messenger, the herald, master of all wealth, Well skilled in this our sacrifice.

samhitapatha: अ॒ग्निम॑ग्निं॒ हवी॑मभिः॒ सदा॑ हवन्त वि॒श्पति॑म् । ह॒व्य॒वाहं॑ पुरुप्रि॒यम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: With callings ever they invoke Agni, Agni, Lord of the House, Oblation-bearer, much beloved.

samhitapatha: अग्ने॑ दे॒वाँ इ॒हा व॑ह जज्ञा॒नो वृ॒क्तब॑र्हिषे । असि॒ होता॑ न॒ ईड्यः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Bring the Gods hither, Agni, born for him who strews the sacred grass: Thou art our herald, meet for praise.

samhitapatha: ताँ उ॑श॒तो वि बो॑धय॒ यद॑ग्ने॒ यासि॑ दू॒त्य॑म् । दे॒वैरा स॑त्सि ब॒र्हिषि॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Wake up the willing Gods, since thou, Agni, performest embassage: Sit on the sacred grass with Gods.

samhitapatha: घृता॑हवन दीदिवः॒ प्रति॑ ष्म॒ रिष॑तो दह । अग्ने॒ त्वं र॑क्ष॒स्विनः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Agni, radiant One, to whom the holy oil is poured, burn up Our enemies whom fiends protect.

samhitapatha: अ॒ग्निना॒ग्निः समि॑ध्यते क॒विर्गृ॒हप॑ति॒र्युवा॑ । ह॒व्य॒वाड्जु॒ह्वा॑स्यः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: By Agni Agni is inflamed, Lord of the House, wise, young, who bears The gift: the ladle is his mouth.

samhitapatha: क॒विम॒ग्निमुप॑ स्तुहि स॒त्यध॑र्माणमध्व॒रे । दे॒वम॑मीव॒चात॑नम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Praise Agni in the sacrifice, the Sage whose ways are ever true, The God who driveth grief away.

samhitapatha: यस्त्वाम॑ग्ने ह॒विष्प॑तिर्दू॒तं दे॑व सप॒र्यति॑ । तस्य॑ स्म प्रावि॒ता भ॑व ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: God, Agni, be his strong defence who lord of sacrificial gifts, Worshippeth thee the messenger.

samhitapatha: यो अ॒ग्निं दे॒ववी॑तये ह॒विष्माँ॑ आ॒विवा॑सति । तस्मै॑ पावक मृळय ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Whoso with sacred gift would fain call Agni to the feast of Gods, O Purifier, favour him.

samhitapatha: स नः॑ पावक दीदि॒वोऽग्ने॑ दे॒वाँ इ॒हा व॑ह । उप॑ य॒ज्ञं ह॒विश्च॑ नः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Such, Agni, Purifier, bright, bring hither to our sacrifice, To our oblation bring the Gods.

samhitapatha: स नः॒ स्तवा॑न॒ आ भ॑र गाय॒त्रेण॒ नवी॑यसा । र॒यिं वी॒रव॑ती॒मिष॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: So lauded by our newest song of praise bring opulence to us, And food, with heroes for our sons.

samhitapatha: अग्ने॑ शु॒क्रेण॑ शो॒चिषा॒ विश्वा॑भिर्दे॒वहू॑तिभिः । इ॒मं स्तोमं॑ जुषस्व नः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Agni, by effulgent flame, by all invokings of the Gods, Show pleasure in this laud of ours.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: सुस॑मिद्धो न॒ आ व॑ह दे॒वाँ अ॑ग्ने ह॒विष्म॑ते । होतः॑ पावक॒ यक्षि॑ च ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: AGNI, well-kindled, bring the Gods for him who offers holy gifts. Worship them, Purifier, Priest.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: मधु॑मन्तं तनूनपाद्य॒ज्ञं दे॒वेषु॑ नः कवे । अ॒द्या कृ॑णुहि वी॒तये॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Son of Thyself, present, O Sage, our sacrifice to the Gods today. Sweet to the taste, that they may feast.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: नरा॒शंस॑मि॒ह प्रि॒यम॒स्मिन्य॒ज्ञ उप॑ ह्वये । मधु॑जिह्वं हवि॒ष्कृत॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Dear Narāśaṁsa, sweet of tongue, the giver of oblations, I Invoke to this our sacrifice.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: अग्ने॑ सु॒खत॑मे॒ रथे॑ दे॒वाँ ई॑ळि॒त आ व॑ह । असि॒ होता॒ मनु॑र्हितः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Agni, on thy most easy car, glorified, hither bring the Gods: Manu appointed thee as Priest.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: स्तृ॒णी॒त ब॒र्हिरा॑नु॒षग्घृ॒तपृ॑ष्ठं मनीषिणः । यत्रा॒मृत॑स्य॒ चक्ष॑णम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Strew, O ye wise, the sacred grass that drips with oil, in order due, Where the Immortal is beheld.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: वि श्र॑यन्तामृता॒वृधो॒ द्वारो॑ दे॒वीर॑स॒श्चतः॑ । अ॒द्या नू॒नं च॒ यष्ट॑वे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Thrown open be the Doors Divine, unfailing, that assist the rite, For sacrifice this day and now.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: नक्तो॒षासा॑ सु॒पेश॑सा॒स्मिन्य॒ज्ञ उप॑ ह्वये । इ॒दं नो॑ ब॒र्हिरा॒सदे॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: I call the lovely Night and Dawn to seat them on the holy grass At this our solemn sacrifice.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: ता सु॑जि॒ह्वा उप॑ ह्वये॒ होता॑रा॒ दैव्या॑ क॒वी । य॒ज्ञं नो॑ यक्षतामि॒मम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The two Invokers I invite, the wise, divine and sweet of tongue, To celebrate this our sacrifice.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: इळा॒ सर॑स्वती म॒ही ति॒स्रो दे॒वीर्म॑यो॒भुवः॑ । ब॒र्हिः सी॑दन्त्व॒स्रिधः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Iḷā, Sarasvatī, Mahī, three Goddesses who bring delight, Be seated, peaceful, on the grass.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: इ॒ह त्वष्टा॑रमग्रि॒यं वि॒श्वरू॑प॒मुप॑ ह्वये । अ॒स्माक॑मस्तु॒ केव॑लः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Tvaṣṭar I call, the earliest born, the wearer of all forms at will: May he be ours and ours alone.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: अव॑ सृजा वनस्पते॒ देव॑ दे॒वेभ्यो॑ ह॒विः । प्र दा॒तुर॑स्तु॒ चेत॑नम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: God, Sovran of the Wood, present this our oblation to the Gods, And let the giver be renowned.

deity: (आप्रीसूक्तं, अग्निरूपा देवता देवताः) १ इध्मः समिद्धोऽग्निर्वा, २ तनूनपात्, ३
samhitapatha: स्वाहा॑ य॒ज्ञं कृ॑णोत॒नेन्द्रा॑य॒ यज्व॑नो गृ॒हे । तत्र॑ दे॒वाँ उप॑ ह्वये ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: With Svāhā pay the sacrifice to Indra in the offerer's house: Thither I call the Deities.

samhitapatha: ऐभि॑रग्ने॒ दुवो॒ गिरो॒ विश्वे॑भिः॒ सोम॑पीतये । दे॒वेभि॑र्याहि॒ यक्षि॑ च ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To drink the Soma, Agni, come, to our service and our songs. With all these Gods; and worship them.

samhitapatha: आ त्वा॒ कण्वा॑ अहूषत गृ॒णन्ति॑ विप्र ते॒ धियः॑ । दे॒वेभि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The Kaṇvas have invoked thee; they, O Singer, sing thee songs of praise Agni, come hither with the Gods;

samhitapatha: इ॒न्द्र॒वा॒यू बृह॒स्पतिं॑ मि॒त्राग्निं पू॒षणं॒ भग॑म् । आ॒दि॒त्यान्मारु॑तं ग॒णम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra, Vāyu, Bṛhaspati, Mitra, Agni, Pūṣan, Bhaga, Ādityas, and the Marut host.

samhitapatha: प्र वो॑ भ्रियन्त॒ इन्द॑वो मत्स॒रा मा॑दयि॒ष्णवः॑ । द्र॒प्सा मध्व॑श्चमू॒षदः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: For you these juices are poured forth that gladden and exhilarate, The meath-drops resting in the cup.

samhitapatha: ईळ॑ते॒ त्वाम॑व॒स्यवः॒ कण्वा॑सो वृ॒क्तब॑र्हिषः । ह॒विष्म॑न्तो अरं॒कृतः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The sons of Kaṇva fain for help adore thee, having strewn the grass, With offerings and all things prepared.

samhitapatha: घृ॒तपृ॑ष्ठा मनो॒युजो॒ ये त्वा॒ वह॑न्ति॒ वह्न॑यः । आ दे॒वान्त्सोम॑पीतये ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Let the swift steeds who carry thee, thought-yoked and dropping holy oil, Bring the Gods to the Soma draught.

samhitapatha: तान्यज॑त्राँ ऋता॒वृधोऽग्ने॒ पत्नी॑वतस्कृधि । मध्वः॑ सुजिह्व पायय ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Adored, the strengtheners of Law, unite them, Agni, with their Dames: Make them drink meath, O bright of tongue.

samhitapatha: ये यज॑त्रा॒ य ईड्या॒स्ते ते॑ पिबन्तु जि॒ह्वया॑ । मधो॑रग्ने॒ वष॑ट्कृति ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Let them, O Agni, who deserve worship and praise drink with thy tongue The meath in solemn sacrifice.

samhitapatha: आकीं॒ सूर्य॑स्य रोच॒नाद्विश्वा॑न्दे॒वाँ उ॑ष॒र्बुधः॑ । विप्रो॒ होते॒ह व॑क्षति ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Away, from the Sun's realm of light, the wise invoking Priest shall bring All Gods awaking with the dawn.

samhitapatha: विश्वे॑भिः सो॒म्यं मध्वग्न॒ इन्द्रे॑ण वा॒युना॑ । पिबा॑ मि॒त्रस्य॒ धाम॑भिः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: With all the Gods, with Indra, with Vāyu, and Mitra's splendours, drink, Agni, the pleasant Soma juice.

samhitapatha: त्वं होता॒ मनु॑र्हि॒तोऽग्ने॑ य॒ज्ञेषु॑ सीदसि । सेमं नो॑ अध्व॒रं य॑ज ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Ordained by Manu as our Priest, thou sittest, Agni, at each rite: Hallow thou this our sacrifice.

samhitapatha: यु॒क्ष्वा ह्यरु॑षी॒ रथे॑ ह॒रितो॑ देव रो॒हितः॑ । ताभि॑र्दे॒वाँ इ॒हा व॑ह ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Harness the Red Mares to thy car, the Bays, O God, the flaming ones: With those bring hitherward the Gods.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: इन्द्र॒ सोमं॒ पिब॑ ऋ॒तुना त्वा॑ विश॒न्त्विन्द॑वः । म॒त्स॒रास॒स्तदो॑कसः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O INDRA drink the Soma juice with Ṛtu; let the cheering drops Sink deep within, which settle there.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: मरु॑तः॒ पिब॑त ऋ॒तुना॑ पो॒त्राद्य॒ज्ञं पु॑नीतन । यू॒यं हि ष्ठा सु॑दानवः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Drink from the Purifier's cup, Maruts, with Ṛtu; sanctify The rite, for ye give precious gifts.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: अ॒भि य॒ज्ञं गृ॑णीहि नो॒ ग्नावो॒ नेष्टः॒ पिब॑ ऋ॒तुना॑ । त्वं हि र॑त्न॒धा असि॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Neṣṭar, with thy Dame accept our sacrifice; with Ṛtu drink, For thou art he who giveth wealth.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: अग्ने॑ दे॒वाँ इ॒हा व॑ह सा॒दया॒ योनि॑षु त्रि॒षु । परि॑ भूष॒ पिब॑ ऋ॒तुना॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Bring the Gods, Agni; in the three appointed places set them down: Surround them, and with Ṛtu drink.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: ब्राह्म॑णादिन्द्र॒ राध॑सः॒ पिबा॒ सोम॑मृ॒तूँरनु॑ । तवेद्धि स॒ख्यमस्तृ॑तम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Drink Soma after the Ṛtus, from the Brāhmaṇa's bounty: undissolved, O Indra, is thy friendship's bond.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: यु॒वं दक्षं॑ धृतव्रत॒ मित्रा॑वरुण दू॒ळभ॑म् । ऋ॒तुना॑ य॒ज्ञमा॑शाथे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Mitra, Varuṇa, ye whose ways are firma Power that none deceives, With Ṛtu ye have reached the rite.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: द्र॒वि॒णो॒दा द्रवि॑णसो॒ ग्राव॑हस्तासो अध्व॒रे । य॒ज्ञेषु॑ दे॒वमी॑ळते ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The Soma-pressers, fain for wealth, praise the Wealth-giver in the rite, In sacrifices praise the God.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: द्र॒वि॒णो॒दा द॑दातु नो॒ वसू॑नि॒ यानि॑ शृण्वि॒रे । दे॒वेषु॒ ता व॑नामहे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: May the Wealth-giver grant to us riches that shall be far renowned. These things we gain, among the Gods.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: द्र॒वि॒णो॒दाः पि॑पीषति जु॒होत॒ प्र च॑ तिष्ठत । ने॒ष्ट्रादृ॒तुभि॑रिष्यत ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: He with the Ṛtu fain would drink, Wealth-giver, from the Neṣṭar's bowl. Haste, give your offering, and depart.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: यत्त्वा॑ तु॒रीय॑मृ॒तुभि॒र्द्रवि॑णोदो॒ यजा॑महे । अध॑ स्मा नो द॒दिर्भ॑व ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: As we this fourth time, Wealth-giver, honour thee with the Ṛtus, be A Giver bountiful to us.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: अश्वि॑ना॒ पिब॑तं॒ मधु॒ दीद्य॑ग्नी शुचिव्रता । ऋ॒तुना॑ यज्ञवाहसा ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Drink ye the meath, O Aśvins bright with flames, whose acts are pure, who with Ṛtus accept the sacrifice.

deity: (प्रतिदैवतं ऋतुसहितम्), १ इन्द्रः, २ मरुतः, ३ त्वष्टा, ४ अग्निः, ५ इन्द्रः, ६
samhitapatha: गार्ह॑पत्येन सन्त्य ऋ॒तुना॑ यज्ञ॒नीर॑सि । दे॒वान्दे॑वय॒ते य॑ज ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: With Ṛtu, through the house-fire, thou, kind Giver, guidest sacrifice: Worship the Gods for the pious man.

samhitapatha: आ त्वा॑ वहन्तु॒ हर॑यो॒ वृष॑णं॒ सोम॑पीतये । इन्द्र॑ त्वा॒ सूर॑चक्षसः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: LET thy Bay Steeds bring thee, the Strong, hither to drink the Soma draught Those, Indra, who are bright as suns.

samhitapatha: इ॒मा धा॒ना घृ॑त॒स्नुवो॒ हरी॑ इ॒होप॑ वक्षतः । इन्द्रं॑ सु॒खत॑मे॒ रथे॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Here are the grains bedewed with oil: hither let the Bay Coursers bring Indra upon his easiest car.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रं॑ प्रा॒तर्ह॑वामह॒ इन्द्रं॑ प्रय॒त्य॑ध्व॒रे । इन्द्रं॒ सोम॑स्य पी॒तये॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra at early morn we call, Indra in course of sacrifice, Indra to drink the Soma juice.

samhitapatha: उप॑ नः सु॒तमा ग॑हि॒ हरि॑भिरिन्द्र के॒शिभिः॑ । सु॒ते हि त्वा॒ हवा॑महे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Come hither, with thy long-maned Steeds, O Indra, to he draught we pour We call thee when the juice is shed.

samhitapatha: सेमं नः॒ स्तोम॒मा ग॒ह्युपे॒दं सव॑नं सु॒तम् । गौ॒रो न तृ॑षि॒तः पि॑ब ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Come thou to this our song of praise, to the libation poured for thee Drink of it like a stag athirst.

samhitapatha: इ॒मे सोमा॑स॒ इन्द॑वः सु॒तासो॒ अधि॑ ब॒र्हिषि॑ । ताँ इ॑न्द्र॒ सह॑से पिब ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Here are the drops of Soma juice expressed on sacred grass: thereof Drink, Indra, to increase thy might.

samhitapatha: अ॒यं ते॒ स्तोमो॑ अग्रि॒यो हृ॑दि॒स्पृग॑स्तु॒ शंत॑मः । अथा॒ सोमं॑ सु॒तं पि॑ब ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Welcome to thee be this our hymn, reaching thy heart, most excellent: Then drink the Soma juice expressed.

samhitapatha: विश्व॒मित्सव॑नं सु॒तमिन्द्रो॒ मदा॑य गच्छति । वृ॒त्र॒हा सोम॑पीतये ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To every draught of pressed-out juice Indra, the Vṛtra-slayer, comes, To drink the Soma for delight.

samhitapatha: सेमं नः॒ काम॒मा पृ॑ण॒ गोभि॒रश्वैः॑ शतक्रतो । स्तवा॑म त्वा स्वा॒ध्यः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Fulfil, O Śatakratu, all our wish with horses and with kine: With holy thoughts we sing thy praise.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रा॒वरु॑णयोर॒हं स॒म्राजो॒रव॒ आ वृ॑णे । ता नो॑ मृळात ई॒दृशे॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: I CRAVE help from the Imperial Lords, from Indra-Varuṇa; may they Both favour one of us like me.

samhitapatha: गन्ता॑रा॒ हि स्थोऽव॑से॒ हवं॒ विप्र॑स्य॒ माव॑तः । ध॒र्तारा॑ चर्षणी॒नाम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Guardians of men, ye ever come with ready succour at the call Of every singer such as I.

samhitapatha: अ॒नु॒का॒मं त॑र्पयेथा॒मिन्द्रा॑वरुण रा॒य आ । ता वां॒ नेदि॑ष्ठमीमहे ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Sate you, according to your wish, O Indra-Varuṇa, with wealth: Fain would we have you nearest us.

samhitapatha: यु॒वाकु॒ हि शची॑नां यु॒वाकु॑ सुमती॒नाम् । भू॒याम॑ वाज॒दाव्ना॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: May we be sharers of the powers, sharers of the benevolence Of you who give strength bounteously.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रः॑ सहस्र॒दाव्नां॒ वरु॑णः॒ शंस्या॑नाम् । क्रतु॑र्भवत्यु॒क्थ्यः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Indra and Varuṇa, among givers of thousands, meet for praise, Are Powers who merit highest laud.

samhitapatha: तयो॒रिदव॑सा व॒यं स॒नेम॒ नि च॑ धीमहि । स्यादु॒त प्र॒रेच॑नम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Through their protection may we gain great store of wealth, and heap it up Enough and still to spare, be ours.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रा॑वरुण वाम॒हं हु॒वे चि॒त्राय॒ राध॑से । अ॒स्मान्त्सु जि॒ग्युष॑स्कृतम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra-Varuṇa, on you for wealth in many a form I call: Still keep ye us victorious.

samhitapatha: इन्द्रा॑वरुण॒ नू नु वां॒ सिषा॑सन्तीषु धी॒ष्वा । अ॒स्मभ्यं॒ शर्म॑ यच्छतम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra-Varuṇa, through our songs that seek to win you to ourselves, Give us at once your sheltering help.

samhitapatha: प्र वा॑मश्नोतु सुष्टु॒तिरिन्द्रा॑वरुण॒ यां हु॒वे । यामृ॒धाथे॑ स॒धस्तु॑तिम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O Indra-Varuṇa, to you may fair praise which I offer come, Joint eulogy which ye dignify.

samhitapatha: सो॒मानं॒ स्वर॑णं कृणु॒हि ब्र॑ह्मणस्पते । क॒क्षीव॑न्तं॒ य औ॑शि॒जः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: O BRĀHMAṆASPATI, make him who presses Soma glorious, Even Kakṣīvān Auśija.

samhitapatha: यो रे॒वान्यो अ॑मीव॒हा व॑सु॒वित्पु॑ष्टि॒वर्ध॑नः । स नः॑ सिषक्तु॒ यस्तु॒रः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The rich, the healer of disease, who giveth wealth, increaseth store, The prompt,may he be with us still.

samhitapatha: मा नः॒ शंसो॒ अर॑रुषो धू॒र्तिः प्रण॒ङ्मर्त्य॑स्य । रक्षा॑ णो ब्रह्मणस्पते ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Let not the foeman's curse, let not a mortal's onslaught fall on us Preserve us, Brahmaṇaspati.

deity: १-३ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः, ४ इन्द्रो ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोमश्च, ५ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोम इंद्रो, दक्षिणा
samhitapatha: स घा॑ वी॒रो न रि॑ष्यति॒ यमिन्द्रो॒ ब्रह्म॑ण॒स्पतिः॑ । सोमो॑ हि॒नोति॒ मर्त्य॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Neer is the mortal hero harmed whom Indra, Brahmaṇaspati, And Soma graciously inspire.

deity: १-३ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः, ४ इन्द्रो ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोमश्च, ५ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोम इंद्रो, दक्षिणा
samhitapatha: त्वं तं ब्र॑ह्मणस्पते॒ सोम॒ इन्द्र॑श्च॒ मर्त्य॑म् । दक्षि॑णा पा॒त्वंह॑सः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Do, thou, O Brahmaṇaspati, and Indra, Soma, Dakṣiṇā, Preserve that mortal from distress.

deity: १-३ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः, ४ इन्द्रो ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोमश्च, ५ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोम इंद्रो, दक्षिणा
samhitapatha: सद॑स॒स्पति॒मद्भु॑तं प्रि॒यमिन्द्र॑स्य॒ काम्य॑म् । स॒निं मे॒धाम॑यासिषम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To the Assembly's wondrous Lord, to Indra's lovely Friend who gives Wisdom, have I drawn near in prayer.

deity: १-३ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः, ४ इन्द्रो ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोमश्च, ५ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोम इंद्रो, दक्षिणा
samhitapatha: यस्मा॑दृ॒ते न सिध्य॑ति य॒ज्ञो वि॑प॒श्चित॑श्च॒न । स धी॒नां योग॑मिन्वति ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: He without whom no sacrifice, een of the wise man, prospers; he Stirs up the series of thoughts.

deity: १-३ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः, ४ इन्द्रो ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोमश्च, ५ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोम इंद्रो, दक्षिणा
samhitapatha: आदृ॑ध्नोति ह॒विष्कृ॑तिं॒ प्राञ्चं॑ कृणोत्यध्व॒रम् । होत्रा॑ दे॒वेषु॑ गच्छति ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: He makes the oblation prosper, he promotes the course of sacrifice: Our voice of praise goes to the Gods.

deity: १-३ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः, ४ इन्द्रो ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोमश्च, ५ ब्रह्मणस्पतिः सोम इंद्रो, दक्षिणा
samhitapatha: नरा॒शंसं॑ सु॒धृष्ट॑म॒मप॑श्यं स॒प्रथ॑स्तमम् । दि॒वो न सद्म॑मखसम् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: I have seen Narāśaṁsa, him most resolute, most widely famed, As twere the Household Priest of heaven.

samhitapatha: प्रति॒ त्यं चारु॑मध्व॒रं गो॑पी॒थाय॒ प्र हू॑यसे । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: To this fair sacrifice to drink the milky draught thou art invoked: O Agni, with the Maruts come.

samhitapatha: न॒हि दे॒वो न मर्त्यो॑ म॒हस्तव॒ क्रतुं॑ प॒रः । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: No mortal man, no God exceeds thy mental power, O Mighty one: O Agni, with the Maruts come:

samhitapatha: ये म॒हो रज॑सो वि॒दुर्विश्वे॑ दे॒वासो॑ अ॒द्रुहः॑ । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: All Gods devoid of guile, who know the mighty region of mid-air: O Agni, with those Maruts come.

samhitapatha: य उ॒ग्रा अ॒र्कमा॑नृ॒चुरना॑धृष्टास॒ ओज॑सा । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The terrible, who sing their song, not to be overcome by might: O Agni, with those Maruts come.

samhitapatha: ये शु॒भ्रा घो॒रव॑र्पसः सुक्ष॒त्रासो॑ रि॒शाद॑सः । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Brilliant, and awful in their form, mighty, devourers of their foes: O Agni, with those Maruts come.

samhitapatha: ये नाक॒स्याधि॑ रोच॒ने दि॒वि दे॒वास॒ आस॑ते । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Who sit as Deities in heaven, above the sky-vault's luminous sphere: O Agni, with those Maruts come.

samhitapatha: य ई॒ङ्खय॑न्ति॒ पर्व॑तान्ति॒रः स॑मु॒द्रम॑र्ण॒वम् । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Who scatter clouds about the sky, away over the billowy sea: O Agni, with those Maruts come.

samhitapatha: आ ये त॒न्वन्ति॑ र॒श्मिभि॑स्ति॒रः स॑मु॒द्रमोज॑सा । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Who with their bright beams spread them forth over the ocean in their might O Agni, with those Maruts come.

samhitapatha: अ॒भि त्वा॑ पू॒र्वपी॑तये सृ॒जामि॑ सो॒म्यं मधु॑ । म॒रुद्भि॑रग्न॒ आ ग॑हि ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: For thee, to be thine early draught, I pour the Soma-mingled meath: O Agni, with the Maruts come.

samhitapatha: अ॒यं दे॒वाय॒ जन्म॑ने॒ स्तोमो॒ विप्रे॑भिरास॒या । अका॑रि रत्न॒धात॑मः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: FOR the Celestial Race this song of praise which gives wealth lavishly Was made by singers with their lips.

samhitapatha: य इन्द्रा॑य वचो॒युजा॑ तत॒क्षुर्मन॑सा॒ हरी॑ । शमी॑भिर्य॒ज्ञमा॑शत ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: They who for Indra, with their mind, formed horses harnessed by a word, Attained by works to sacrifice.

samhitapatha: तक्ष॒न्नास॑त्याभ्यां॒ परि॑ज्मानं सु॒खं रथ॑म् । तक्ष॑न्धे॒नुं स॑ब॒र्दुघा॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: They for the two Nāsatyas wrought a light car moving every way: They formed a nectar-yielding cow.

samhitapatha: युवा॑ना पि॒तरा॒ पुनः॑ स॒त्यम॑न्त्रा ऋजू॒यवः॑ । ऋ॒भवो॑ वि॒ष्ट्य॑क्रत ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The Ṛbhus with effectual prayers, honest, with constant labour, made Their Sire and Mother young again.

samhitapatha: सं वो॒ मदा॑सो अग्म॒तेन्द्रे॑ण च म॒रुत्व॑ता । आ॒दि॒त्येभि॑श्च॒ राज॑भिः ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Together came your gladdening drops with Indra by the Maruts girt, With the Ādityas, with the Kings.

samhitapatha: उ॒त त्यं च॑म॒सं नवं॒ त्वष्टु॑र्दे॒वस्य॒ निष्कृ॑तम् । अक॑र्त च॒तुरः॒ पुनः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: The sacrificial ladle, wrought newly by the God Tvaṣṭar's hand Four ladles have ye made thereof.

samhitapatha: ते नो॒ रत्ना॑नि धत्तन॒ त्रिरा साप्ता॑नि सुन्व॒ते । एक॑मेकं सुश॒स्तिभिः॑ ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: Vouchsafe us wealth, to him who pours thrice seven libations, yea, to each Give wealth, pleased with our eulogies.

samhitapatha: अधा॑रयन्त॒ वह्न॒योऽभ॑जन्त सुकृ॒त्यया॑ । भा॒गं दे॒वेषु॑ य॒ज्ञिय॑म् ॥

translation.author: Ralph T.H. Griffith
translation.fluid_text: As ministering Priests they held, by pious acts they won themselves, A share in sacrifice with Gods.
